task_completion.description: Verify that the ReqView application is open and the Example Project is loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ReqView application window is visible, and the project tree or title indicates the Example Project is loaded.

task_completion.description: Verify that the ARCH (Architecture Design) document is open and active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'ARCH' document is selected in the left pane or visible as the active tab/view in the main content area.

task_completion.description: Verify that a top-level section titled 'Performance Constraints' has been added.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A heading with the text 'Performance Constraints' is visible in the document. It should be at the top level of the hierarchy (e.g., numbered as a main section like '6' or '7', not a subsection).

task_completion.description: Verify that a child requirement has been added under 'Performance Constraints' with the correct text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A requirement is nested directly under the 'Performance Constraints' heading (indented or numbered as a sub-item like '6.1'). Its text must include 'real-time', '50 milliseconds', and 'sensor input'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the project has been saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save icon/button in the toolbar is grayed out or inactive, or there is no unsaved changes indicator (like an asterisk) next to the project or document name.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the ReqView graphical user interface to make the changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show interaction with the ReqView application menus, buttons, and text fields, rather than a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not directly edit the ARCH.json file using a text editor or terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) or terminal windows showing file manipulation commands (like sed, echo, jq) are visible modifying the project files.

integrity.description: Verify that the requirement text was entered into the ReqView application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text appears within the ReqView document view, properly formatted as a requirement, not just as plain text in a terminal or unrelated application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target document is 'ARCH' (Architecture Design). The top-level heading must be exactly 'Performance Constraints'. The child requirement must be a direct child (hierarchy depth 1) and its text must include 'real-time', '50 milliseconds', and 'sensor input'.
